name: Reinsurance Professional
description: Memory structure for reinsurance industry professionals. Covers your role in the reinsurance value chain, compliance requirements, treaty/program context, and industry-specific tools.

memories.name: Reinsurance Role
memories.description: Your position in the reinsurance value chain, lines of business, and key relationships
content_prompts: What is your name, title, and organization (carrier, broker, MGA, cedent)?
What lines of business do you focus on (property cat, casualty, specialty, life)?
Where do you sit in the value chain (underwriting, actuarial, claims, broking)?
Who are your key counterparties (cedents, retrocessionaires, brokers)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: user
name: Reinsurance Role
description: Position in the reinsurance market, lines of business, and key counterparties
---

- Name: Michael Torres, VP Treaty Underwriting at Helvetia Re
- Lines: property catastrophe, per-risk property, casualty clash
- Role: price and structure treaty placements, manage portfolio 
- Key relationships: 12 cedents via Guy Carpenter and Aon, 3 retro providers

memories.name: Compliance Requirements
memories.description: Regulatory constraints, reporting standards, and approval workflows that govern your work
content_prompts: What regulatory frameworks govern your work (Solvency II, NAIC, Bermuda BMA, Lloyd's)?
What reporting standards do you follow (IFRS 17, US GAAP, SAP)?
What are the key compliance checkpoints in your treaty placement or claims workflow?
Are there specific documentation or audit trail requirements for your jurisdiction?
What approval chains exist for binding authority, limit deployment, or reserve changes?
memories.example_content:
---
type: feedback
name: Compliance Requirements
description: Regulatory frameworks, reporting standards, and approval workflows
---

- Regulated under Solvency II (EU) and Swiss FINMA
- Reporting: IFRS 17 for group, local GAAP for Swiss entity
- Treaty workflow: pricing sign-off (actuarial), terms approval (CUO), capacity check (CFO for >$50M)
- Audit: all treaty terms must be documented in placement file within 30 days of binding
- Sanctions screening required on all new cedent relationships

memories.name: Treaty and Program Context
memories.description: Active treaty programs, renewal pipeline, and portfolio composition you manage
content_prompts: What is your current portfolio size (GWP, number of treaties, key programs)?
What renewals are upcoming and what are the strategic priorities for them?
Are there any new programs or cedent relationships you are developing?
What is the current loss experience or reserve position on your key programs?
What cat model outputs or pricing metrics are most relevant to your book?
memories.example_content:
---
type: project
name: Treaty and Program Context
description: Active treaties, renewal pipeline, and portfolio priorities
---

- Portfolio: $320M GWP across 45 treaties, 65% property cat / 35% casualty
- Upcoming: Jan 1 renewals (18 treaties), targeting 5% rate increase on prop cat
- New business: exploring Southeast Asia cedents via Gallagher Re
- Loss experience: 2024 Hurricane season — $45M incurred across 3 programs
- Key metrics: RoL, technical price ratio, PML at 250yr RP (RMS and AIR)

memories.name: Industry Tools and Platforms
memories.description: Reinsurance-specific software, cat modeling platforms, and data systems you use
content_prompts: What cat modeling platforms do you use (RMS, AIR, Verisk, in-house)?
What underwriting or policy admin systems does your organization run?
What data platforms or actuarial tools support your analysis (Arius, ResQ, Excel models)?
What market platforms do you interact with (Riskonnect, Sequel, PPL, ACORD messaging)?
Where do you access industry data (PCS, Swiss Re sigma, AM Best)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: reference
name: Industry Tools and Platforms
description: Cat models, underwriting systems, and market platforms used in reinsurance work
---

- Cat models: RMS RiskLink 23.0 (primary), AIR Touchstone (validation), custom blend model
- UW system: in-house treaty management platform (TreatyHub) for pricing and tracking
- Actuarial: Arius for reserving, custom Python models for experience rating
- Market: Riskonnect for placement tracking, ACORD XML for bordereaux exchange
- Data: PCS loss bulletins, Swiss Re sigma, AM Best for cedent ratings
